observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to review their records and locate the foregoing variances with a net amount of P1,829,837.90 and effect the necessary adjustment. Henceforth, instruct the latter. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile their records to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the reported balances, hence cash account will be fairly and accurately presented in the FS of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to reconcile his records with the Municipal Treasurer due to the untimely passing of the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Verification of records revealed that the discrepancies between the records of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remained unreconciled due to the untimely passing of the former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and monitoring of the receivable accounts, we recommended that:
a. The LCE instruct the Accounting Office and the Implementing Office/s to intensify efforts on the collection of the long outstanding receivables and seek legal remedies, if necessary;
b. The LCE file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16,2016, especially those recognized in the books as impaired, if not possible, instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the specific receivables in the Notes to FS; and
c. The Municipal Accountant monitor and analyze on a regular basis, all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable.
observations.managementAction: The finance department justified to start sending demand letters this year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 44 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
a. instruct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory and reconcile the results thereon with the accounting records to ascertain the accuracy, completeness and existence of the reported PPE in the financial statement;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the hereunder adjusting journal entry for the reclassification of the recorded Shredding Machine: Debit: Machinery 47,994,695.29 Credit Construction and Heavy Equipment 47,994,695.29; and
c. instruct the Property Officer, the Head of Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 for the conduct of physical count of properties; recognition of PPE items found at station; disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items; and for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, in order to reflect more reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts, hence to convey precise information useful in decision making and accountability for the properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was not able to reconcile his records with the Municipal Treasurer due to the untimely and Municipal Treasurer remained unreconciled the Inventory Committee was not able to prioritize the conduct of physical count for the year 2024.
observations.reason: The management was not able to conduct annual physical count of PPE for the year 2024. No report on annual physical inventory count of PPE was submitted to the audit team. Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 46 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of infrastructure assets and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as his basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of the assets to be derecognized from the books in order to fairly and reliably present these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of preparing the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of infrastructure assets.
observations.reason: No action taken by management. Reiterated in Observation No.3, page 48 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Determine the unclaimed retention by money for its possible payment and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance for these projects which will serve as their basis in the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher (DV) even without request for payment from the contractors/supplier, and
b. Make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not to overstate the liability accounts. Likewise, attach Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) and all available records to support the amount of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and a copy shall be attached in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.
observations.managementAction: The municipality is in the process of locating and compiling the necessary documentation and records for the long outstanding liabilities. Reiterated in Observation No. 4, page 50 of the report.
observations.reason: No action taken by management. Reiterated in Observation No. 4, page 50 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials to immediately submit the duly accomplished and certified list of recipients of the procured goods to the Municipal Accountant who will subsequently submit the same to the Auditor to ascertain the completeness and propriety of the same and to ensure that these were actually received by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of completing the necessary documents. The duly accomplished and certified list of recipients was not yet submitted to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.reason: The management is still in the process of completing the necessary documents. The duly accomplished and certified list of recipients was not yet submitted to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure the required document and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant thus, facilitating the immediate submission of the lacking documents as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to substantiate the propriety, legality and validity of the acquired land.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of securing the documentary requirements. Reiterated in Observation No. 7, page 68 of the report.
observations.reason: These parcels of land remained untitled as of 2024. However, Management is in the process of securing a special patent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct General Services Officer (GSO) to require the drivers to completely accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 to provide basis in determining the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking documents in order to substantiate the legality, validity and propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is in the process of implementing measures to require the drivers to completely accomplish Driver's Trip Tickets and to facilitate submission of supporting documentation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 15, page 68 of the report. No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from utilizing the collections to encash checks and from using the same for payment of LGU’s other obligations. Deposit the same promptly and intact as to the manner in which they were collected in conformity with Sections 67 (3) and 69 (1) of PD No. 1445, hence to avoid misuse and misappropriation of collections and to ensure safety of the same.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the collections will not be utilized for encashment and will be promptly deposited; however, post-audit showed continued use for check encashment.
observations.reason: Post-audit of collections revealed that collections were still utilized for check encashment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to prioritize the implementation of the projects funded out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents.
Likewise, we recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable development projects that help in the attainment of the purpose and objective of the fund which is to advance the self-reliance of the farmer beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to implement 38 PPAs under RA No. 7171 funds however, 45 projects remained unimplemented. The management is in the process of evaluating unimplemented projects for possible realignment.
observations.reason: Reiterated Observation No. 17, page 78 of the report. No action taken to implement the remaining unimplemented projects; however, 38 PPAs were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC and other concerned Department Heads to revisit relevant documents of the programs/projects funded by NGAs and implement them so that the purpose/s these funds were given will be achieved.
We also recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to determine the date of the fund transfers to accurately present it in the aging schedule in order to accurately determine the date of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The project has already undergone bidding; however, it was not yet started due to shortage of materials. The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of locating the supporting documents in order to determine the date of transfer.
observations.reason: The PPA funded by NGAs remained unimplemented as at year end. The exact date of fund transfer remained unidentified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately enforce the settlement of the audit suspensions of P6,797,382.39 to avoid maturing of the same into disallowance and disallowances of P19,642,709.99 in order not to deprive the Municipal Government of the funds that could be derived therefrom and to fund other priority projects.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer, in compliance with the directives of the issued COE, to cause the collection or settlement of the disallowances directly from the persons liable to avoid its referral to the General Counsel for appropriate action including referral to the Office of the Solicitor General and the Ombudsman.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management. There was no settlement made during 2024. Management reasoned out that significant portion of the suspension pertained to the procured goods during the Covid-19 pandemic through the emergency purchases which was previously allowed under the Bayanihan Act. Nevertheless, Management committed to settle the outstanding suspensions. As regards the disallowances, Management have already filed their appeal proceedings.
observations.reason: No action taken by management. There was no settlement made during 2024. Reiterated in Observation No. 19, page 82 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 For the proper management and monitoring of the receivable accounts,
the Audit Team recommended that:
1.7.1 The LCE instruct the Accounting Office and the Implementing
Office/s to intensify efforts on the collection of the long outstanding
receivables and seek legal remedies, if necessary;
1.7.2 The LCE file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables in
accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016, especially those recognized in the books as impaired, if not possible, instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the specific receivables in the Notes to FS; and
1.7.3 The Municipal Accountant monitor and analyze on a regular basis, all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable.
1.8 We likewise recommended that Management avail of the advantages of the COA Circular 2023-008 by adhering to its provisions for the cleansing or derecognition of dormant receivables from the books of accounts, hence to present fairly the receivable account in the financial statements.

2.6 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct physical annual inventory as required under
Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 124 of MNGAS for
LGUs to ascertain the existence, serviceability and correct valuation of the
properties presented in the financial statement.
2.7 Likewise, instruct the Property Officer, the Head of Inventory Committee
and the Municipal Accountant to avail of the advantages of COA Circular
2020-06 dated January 31, 2020, and adhere with the guidelines and
procedures in the conduct of physical count of properties; recognition of
PPE items found at station; disposition for non-existing/missing PPE
items; and for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, in order to
reflect more reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts, hence to
convey precise information useful in decision making and accountability
for these properties.

3.7 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal
Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information
relative to the replaced parts of infrastructure assets and furnish the same
to the Municipal Accountant to serve as his basis in determining the
appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of the assets to be
derecognized from the books in order to fairly and reliably present these
accounts in the FS.

4.8 In order that the long overdue liabilities will not affect the fair
presentation of the FS, the Audit Team recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
4.8.1 Determine the unclaimed retention money for its possible payment
and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the issuance of
Certificate of Final Acceptance for these projects which will serve
as their basis in the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher (DV)
even without request for payment from the contractors/supplier,
and
4.8.2 Make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the
corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long
outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion in order not
to overstate the liability accounts. Likewise, attach Subsidiary
Ledgers (SL) and all available records to support the amount of the
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and a copy shall be attached in case
of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. If there may be claims in
the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a
supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

5.7 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
5.7.1 Conduct a detailed review of the affected liability accounts to
determine the root cause of the negative balances and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the accounting errors, hence
to present accurately the balances of the affected liability accounts
in the FS; and
5.7.2 Moving forward, establish a sound financial procedure/process to
facilitate proper recording of liability accounts ensuring verifiability
and regular monitoring of the same to establish accurate and
faithful presentation of the payable accounts in the FS.

6.7 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the variance of P364,141.10 with the records of the
Provincial Accountant to ensure correct and accurate balance of the Due to
LGUs account in the FS and to ensure the correctness of the remittances
made, thus FS will be fairly presented.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to secure the ORs from the payees/contractors/service providers of the abovementioned transactions totaling P31,206,408.48 thus payments of obligations were fully acknowledged and the transactions are substantiated and submit the same to the Auditor in order to ascertain the validity, propriety and truthfulness of these transactions.
Henceforth, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to require ORs as evidence of payment of telephone and internet providers as evidence of payment pursuant to the abovementioned BIR regulations, in order to assist the BIR foster its collection of taxes, thus helping the government in the provision of necessary cash requirements for its priority programs, projects and activities that are beneficial to the public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were able to secure and submit to the Audit Team ORs from contractors.
observations.reason: Verification of records revealed that the ORs from contractors were secured and submitted to the Audit Team. However, payment to telephone and internet providers were still directly deposited to their bank accounts. Post-audit of 2024 transactions revealed that payments for telephone and internet services were not substantiated with ORs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. increase the amount of PCF for operating expenditures sufficient for one-month expenses, hence to prevent frequent replenishment of fund, thus lessen the workloads and utilization of resources; and
b. henceforth, monitor and ensure that expenses charged from the PCF are non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses in consonance with Section 8 of the NGAS Manual.
We also recommended that Management cater the needs of the AICS recipients in a timely manner by setting up a separate cash advance exclusively for this purpose, thus to meet the needed resources of the AICS beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The PCF was increased to 130,000.00 during the year. The Municipal Accountant has been monitoring the current receivables to ensure collectability. Payment of AICS continues to be delayed despite increased PCF.
observations.reason: The PCF was increased to 130,000.00 during the year. Expenses charged under the PCF were all petty expenses. However, payment of AICS continues to be delayed despite increased PCF. Post-audit of disbursements revealed that payment of AICS continues to be delayed despite increase of PCF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the provisions of Section 116 and 122 of the NGAS Manual that the Head of Offices or Departments needing the supplies shall prepare the RIS for supplies available in stock and Purchase Request (PR) for supplies that are not available in stock and submit the same to the GSO which would serve as her basis in the requisition and issuance of supplies, hence effecting the proper control in the management of supplies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted JEV and supporting documents of the recorded transaction in the GL to the Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were able to secure ORs from contractors. The PCF was increased and petty expenses monitored.
observations.reason: Post-audit of disbursement vouchers for the year 2024 revealed that the RIS were prepared by head of requisitioning departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting schedules for the remittance of RPT/CTC shares, municipal aid and shares from market operations to the concerned LGUs to avoid the said funds from dormancy; and
b. Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit from RPT/CTC collections and municipal aid promptly/regularly the shares of the Province and Barangays from the RPT/CTC collections in adherence to the provision of Section 271 (d) and 164 (c) of RA 7160, thus helping them augment their financial resources for their operations and development projects.
observations.managementAction: The shares of the province and barangays from the RPT/CTC collection for the 1st to 3rd quarters of 2024 were remitted within the same year.
observations.reason: Verification of the year-end FS and supporting schedules revealed that only the 4th quarter shares from the RPT/CTC collections were unremitted as at year-end. The 1st to 3rd quarter shares were remitted within the same year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unutilized balances to the Bureau of Treasury or return the same to the source agencies to finally end any obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Unremitted residual balances from completed projects in the previous years were returned to the Bureau of Treasury during 2024.
observations.reason: Unremitted residual balances from completed projects in previous years were returned to the Bureau of Treasury during 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately duly certified copies of the journals and the JEV and supporting documents of the transactions recorded in the GL in order to ascertain the validity and propriety of the transactions, especially the recorded payables of P98,507,879.16. Henceforth, we recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant strict compliance on the Journals and ledger in the abovementioned prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted JEV and supporting documents of the recorded transaction in the GL to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: JEV and supporting documents of the recorded transactions in the GL were already submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant submitted journals and ledgers in the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Finance Committee to evaluate thoroughly the feasibility of acquiring loans by undertaking applicable necessary economic evaluation method such as the cost-benefit analysis to ensure that the most advantageous alternative or course of action for the LGU is obtained before resorting into debt financing in order to avoid incurring unnecessary interest charges.
observations.managementAction: The additional loan released in 2024 was based on the application submitted in early 2024, with no further loan applications made afterwards.
observations.reason: The additional loan released in 2024 was based on the application submitted in early 2024, with no further loan applications made afterwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Secretariat to post the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPs on the prescribed period as required in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, in order to conform with the objectives of the procurement law in promoting transparency and accountability in the conduct of procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendation. Review of contracts disclosed that the NOA and NTP of various procurements were posted within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Review of contracts disclosed that the NOA and NTP of various procurements were posted within the reglementary period.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist already submitted the List of Recipients. The GSO has submitted the SSMI and RSI to the Accounting Office to be used as a basis for the JEV preparation.

The GSO has submitted the SSMI and RSI to the Accounting Office to be used as a basis for the JEV preparation. However, liquidation documents for all distributed inventories were not yet complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory and invite the Audit Team or his representative to witness the activity and reconcile the results of the count with the property and accounting records to ascertain the accuracy, completeness, and existence of the recorded PPE accounts in the FS. Likewise, we recommended the LCE to instruct the Inventory Committee to implement the one-time cleansing of accounts prescribed in this COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and derecognize small value properties which are below the capitalization threshold in order not to overstate the PPE accounts and to ascertain the fair presentation of the same in the FS.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory of properties was conducted during year end. The municipality targeted to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE in 2024.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE does not reconcile with the balance of PPE as appearing in the financial statements. The municipality did not yet implement the one-time cleansing of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Inventory Committee to implement the one-time cleansing of accounts prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and derecognize small value properties which are below the capitalization threshold in order not to overstate the PPE accounts and to ascertain the fair presentation of the same in the FS.
observations.managementAction: Small value properties which are below the capitalization threshold were derecognized.
observations.reason: Out of the ₱4,164,813.89 cost of properties below the capitalization threshold, only ₱412,607.47 were derecognized during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake the immediate reconciliation of cash balances between the ledgers and cashbooks so as to ascertain the accuracy and correctness of the account balance of Cash-Local Treasury and for its fair presentation in the FS.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation of records between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant is still ongoing.
observations.reason: The Cash in Local Treasury Account cashbook and ledger has an unreconciled balance amounting to ₱1,829,837.90.

The LCE file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016, especially those recognized in the books as impaired, if not possible, instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the specific receivables in the Notes to FS; and 3. observations.reason: No significant change in the amount of outstanding receivables. The Municipal Accountant is in the process of determining the outstanding receivables and the receivables that needs to be written-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat, as a support staff of the BAC, see to it that the NOA and NTP are posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period of 3 and 15 calendar days in order to ensure procurement transparency.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to post the NOA and NTP of various projects in the PhilGEPS; however, they were able to lessen the delays compared to last year.

observations.reason: There are outstanding balances of NS and ND amounting to P6,797,382.39 and P19,642,709.99, respectively. The Accountant is still waiting for the appeals' decisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption and ensure prompt submission of duly accomplished Report on Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively as provided in Section 124, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs in order to ascertain the condition and completeness of the inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or consumption was conducted during year end. The report on the physical count of supplies for distribution or consumption was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Auditor list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Also, provide a notice to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days, after the award of infrastructure projects or before its start that the project signboard was already posted. We further recommended that upon the final payment of contracts, the Municipal Accountant require attachment of duly approved program to ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 and relevant issuances of the DOLE. Likewise, it is recommended that Management require the refund of the cost for the implementation of CSHP amounting to ₱3,589,812.36 by the concerned contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records be given priority by the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant. Likewise, we recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to ensure prompt submission of duly and completely accomplished RPCPPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make a compilation of any record/documentation retrieved from their ongoing efforts and prepare the corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long outstanding liabilities to support the possible reversion.  Subsidiary ledgers and all available records to support the amount should be attached to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) that has to be prepared in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus. In the event that there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the payable.

For the proper management and monitoring of the receivables accounts, we recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Executive Chief instruct the Accounting Office and the Implementing Office/s to intensify efforts on the collection of the long outstanding receivables and seek for legal remedies, if necessary;
Local Executive Chief file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
The Municipal Accountant monitor and analyze on a regular basis, all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable.

We recommended and Management agreed to see to it that only regular government employees be assigned to perform duties which permit or require the possession, custody or control of local government funds and which also require bonding of their fidelity. Likewise, we recommended that Management carefully examine the need to assign regular employees to other departments or offices. Such assignments should not hamper the performance of their original functions.

We recommended and Management agreed to religiously remit withheld contributions and loan amortizations on time. Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to prioritize and closely monitor the ongoing reconciliation with the GSIS in order not to affect again the benefits of employees who will opt to retire in the future.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in order to substantiate the legality, validity and propriety of transactions and in order to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension. We recommended further and the newly appointed Municipal Accountant agreed to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents for audit purposes.

We recommended and the BAC Members and the TWG agreed to carefully examine and evaluate the documents submitted by the bidders in order to ensure that these complied with and are responsive to all the requirements and conditions as specified in the bidding documents in order to avoid disallowance in audit. It is likewise recommended that the BAC Secretariat require the payments of bidding document fee prior to submission of bid proposals.  Failure of the bidder to comply with the requirements of the BAC should result in disqualification in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to invite at least three observers namely: one (1) representative of the COA, one (1) from a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand, and one (1) from a non-government organization at least five (5) calendar days before the conduct of Pre-Bid Conference in accordance with Sec. 13.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to enhance the transparency of the procurement process.

We recommended and Management agreed that 10% of the contract price representing the retention money should be deducted unless there is duly approved letter request for substitution. If the request for substitution is granted, the surety bond to be posted should be equivalent and for the purpose of the retention money.  Likewise, the Certificate of Final Acceptance should be issued one year after the project completion and posting of warranty security against structural defects and failures, thereby strict monitoring should be made by the Municipal Engineer on these infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Secretariat, as a support staff of the Bids and Awards Committee, agreed to see to it that the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed are posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period of 3 and 15 calendar days in order to ensure procurement transparency.

We recommended that the BAC require the winning bidders/contractors to submit duly approved Construction Safety and Health Program as part of the perfected contracts. We also recommended that pending the approval by the DOLE of the program on the date of submission of perfected contracts, Management may require a certification that the program has been submitted for approval by the DOLE.
We further recommended that upon the final payment of contracts, the Municipal Accountant require attachment of duly approved program to ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 and relevant issuances of the DOLE. Likewise, it is recommended that Management require the refund of the cost for the implementation of CSHP amounting to ₱3,589,812.36 by the concerned contractors.

We recommended that Management, thru the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate standard implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation or AICS to avoid inequitable/discretionary allocation to the constituents of the Municipality. We encouraged Management to consider requiring relevant and valid supporting documents consistent with Memorandum Circular No. 11, Series of 2019 issued by the DSWD which sets forth the revised guidelines on the Implementation of AICS.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to cause the immediate implementation of the funded projects out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure the full implementation of the priority projects which remained unimplemented in 2019 and ensure that 20% Development Funds be optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU in accordance with the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all officials concerned to cause the immediate settlement of all suspensions before these mature to disallowances. We also recommended that all persons liable be required to refund the disallowed amounts to the Municipal Government. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere with the documentation of all disbursement vouchers before effecting payment of all claims and before submission to the Auditor’s Office to avoid suspensions and disallowances. COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 should be used as reference on the documentary requirements for common government transactions.

We recommended that the reconciliation of the results of the count with the property and accounting records be given priority. Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee ensure that the submitted Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment is complete in details.
